summary: Iteration 51: Report Config Refactoring - All 20 backend tests PASSED. Verified Cash Book and Party Ledger exports use shared config, report_config.json structure validated.
critical_code_review_comments: report_config.json correctly extended with cashbook_report (10 columns), party_ledger_report (7 columns), daily_paddy_entries_report (summary_mode + detail_mode)
Python backend cashbook.py properly imports and uses get_columns(), get_entry_row(), get_total_row() from utils/report_helper.py
Python backend ledgers.py properly refactored to use shared config for Party Ledger Excel/PDF exports
Node.js desktop-app/routes/cashbook.js properly imports from shared/report_helper.js
Shared report_helper.py and report_helper.js provide consistent column extraction functions
Excel/PDF exports verified to output correct headers from config: Date, Account, Type, Party, Party Type, Description, Jama, Nikasi, Balance, Reference
context_for_next_testing_agent: Report config refactoring complete. All exports (Cash Book Excel/PDF, Party Ledger Excel/PDF, Private Trading Excel, Agent Mandi) verified working. Shared config at /app/shared/report_config.json contains all report definitions. Python uses /app/backend/utils/report_helper.py, Node.js uses /app/shared/report_helper.js.
